title: Human waste detecting and signaling devices, methods, and systems
abstract: Methods, devices, and systems for human waste detecting and signaling are provided in accordance with various embodiments. For example, some embodiments include a method for human waste detecting and signaling that may include: conveying human waste to a detector located proximal to an edge of a diaper; and/or signaling the presence of the human waste at the detector through producing a colored compound. In some embodiments, conveying the human waste to the detector utilizes micro-capillaries. The human waste may include urea and/or fecal matter. Producing the colored compound may include reacting the urea and/or fecal matter with a chromogenic reagent. Some embodiments include a human waste detecting and signaling device that may include a detector and a conveyor; the conveyor may include micro-capillaries in some embodiments. Some embodiments include a system that may include a diaper along with a human waste detecting and signaling device.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of human waste detecting and signaling comprising: conveying a human waste to a detector located proximal to an edge of a diaper; and signaling the presence of the human waste at the detector through producing a colored compound. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein conveying the human waste to the detector utilizes micro-capillaries. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the human waste includes urea. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein producing the colored compound includes reacting the urea with a chromogenic reagent. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes p- dimethylaminobenzaldehyde. 6. The method of claim 3, wherein producing the colored compound includes reacting the urea with ortho-Phthalaldehyde. 7. The method of claim 6, further comprising reacting at least N-(l- naphthyl)ethylenediamine or antipyrine with one or more reaction products produced from reacting the urea with the ortho-Phthalaldehyde to produce the colored compound. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the detector is positioned to extend beyond the edge of the diaper. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the detector is positioned within the diaper along the edge of the diaper. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the edge of the diaper includes a waist edge of the diaper. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the detector is positioned proximal to a lateral portion of the diaper. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein lateral portion of the diaper includes a leg opening of the diaper. 13. The method of claim 1, further comprising coupling at least the detector or the conveyor with the diaper. 14. The method of claim 1, further comprising integrating at least the detector or the conveyor with the diaper. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein the human waste includes fecal matter. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein producing the colored compound includes reacting one or more compounds of the fecal matter with a chromogenic reagent. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes 7- Nitro-l,2,3-benzoxadiazole. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes para-dimethylaminocinnamaldehyde. 19. A human waste detecting and signaling device comprising: a detector configured to be positioned proximal to an edge of a diaper, wherein the detector produces a colored compound when a human waste is detected at the detector; and a conveyor configured to deliver the human waste from an interior portion of the diaper to the detector. 20. The device of claim 19, wherein the conveyor includes micro- capillaries. 21. The device of claim 19, wherein the human waste includes urea. 22. The device of claim 21, wherein detector includes a chromogenic reagent configured to produce the colored compound when the urea is detected. 23. The device of claim 22, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes p- dimethylaminobenzaldehyde. 24. The device of claim 21, wherein the detector includes ortho- Phthalaldehyde. 25. The device of claim 24, wherein the detector further includes at least N-(l-naphthyl)ethylenediamine or antipyrine. 26. The device of claim 19, wherein the detector is configured to be positioned to extend beyond the edge of the diaper. 27. The device of claim 19, wherein the detector is configured to be positioned within the diaper along the edge of the diaper. 28. The device of claim 19, wherein the edge of the diaper includes a waist edge of the diaper. 29. The device of claim 19, further comprising an adhesive configured to couple at least the detector or the conveyor with the diaper. 30. The device of claim 19, wherein at least the detector or the conveyor is integrated with the diaper. 31. The device of claim 19, wherein the detector is configured to be positioned proximal to a lateral portion of the diaper. 32. The diaper of claim 19, wherein lateral portion of the diaper includes a leg opening of the diaper. 33. The device of claim 19, further comprising the diaper. 34. The device of claim 19, wherein the human waste includes fecal matter. 35. The device of claim 34, wherein detector includes a chromogenic reagent configured to produce the colored compound when the fecal matter is detected. 36. The device of claim 35, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes 7- Nitro-l,2,3-benzoxadiazole. 37. The device of claim 35, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes para-dimethylaminocinnamaldehyde. 38. A human waste detecting and signaling system comprising: a diaper; a detector positioned proximal to an edge of the diaper, wherein the detector produces a colored compound when a human waste is detected at the detector; and a conveyor configured to deliver the human waste from an interior portion of the diaper to the detector. 39. The system of claim 38, wherein the conveyor includes mi cro- capillaries. 40. The system of claim 38, wherein the human waste includes urea. 41. The system of claim 40, wherein the detector includes a chromogenic reagent configured to produce the colored compound when the urea is detected. 42. The system of claim 41, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes p- dimethylaminobenzaldehyde. 43. The system of claim 40, wherein the detector includes ortho- Phthalaldehyde. 44. The system of claim 43, wherein the detector further includes at least N-(l-naphthyl)ethylenediamine or antipyrine. 45. The system of claim 38, wherein the detector is positioned to extend beyond the edge of the diaper. 46. The system of claim 38, wherein the detector is positioned within the diaper along the edge of the diaper. 47. The system of claim 38, wherein the edge of the diaper includes a waist edge of the diaper. 48. The system of claim 38, further comprising an adhesive that couples at least the detector or the conveyor with the diaper. 49. The system of claim 38, wherein at least the detector or the conveyor is integrated with the diaper. 50. The system of claim 38, wherein the detector is positioned proximal to a lateral portion of the diaper. 51. The system of claim 50, wherein lateral portion of the diaper includes a leg opening of the diaper. 52. The system of claim 38, wherein the human waste includes fecal matter. 53. The system of claim 52, wherein detector includes a chromogenic reagent configured to produce the colored compound when the fecal matter is detected. 54. The system of claim 53, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes 7- Nitro-l,2,3-benzoxadiazole. 55. The system of claim 53, wherein the chromogenic reagent includes para-dimethylaminocinnamaldehyde.
